commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The men of Arvad with your army were upon your walls round about, and the Gammadim were in your towers: they hung their shields upon your walls round about; they have made your beauty perfect. The Pygmeans. That is, strong and valiant men. In Hebrew Gamma dim. (Challoner) He does not speak of those fabulous men hardly a cubit high. Gomed signifying a "cubit "has caused them to be styled so here. Septuagint, "guards "or Symmachus, "Medes. "Ezechiel (xxxviii. ) speaks of the Gomerim.
